cases.next_action: Lock one public-seam test that submits the worker job, then calls wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout=bounded_timeout, description="job to complete"). Run it before implementation, make the smallest production change, and refactor only while green.
cases.proof: RED must be an observed timeout or assertion showing the submitted job never reaches complete, not setup failure. GREEN requires the focused command passing through the public seam and the nearest relevant group passing. After each refactor move, rerun the focused test; rerun the nearby group before return.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim TDD completion only with recorded Source Trace, observed expected RED, GREEN, nearby validation, refactor result, and residual risk; deterministic waiting permits no sleep-based claim.

cases.next_action: Observe DEBOUNCE_STARTED, record the fake-clock time, advance to just before DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and prove SEARCH_REQUESTED is absent, then advance the remainder and prove it appears at exactly the contractual instant.
cases.proof: Observed RED must show the timing contract is missing or wrong. GREEN must show no early request and one request exactly one interval after the observed trigger, followed by the nearest relevant test group.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only the specified timing behavior through the fake clock and observable events; do not generalize from elapsed wall-clock sleeps or an assertion that merely proves eventual occurrence.

cases.next_action: Hand off to $diagnosing-bugs because the cause and trusted red-capable reproduction are uncertain.
cases.proof: Diagnosis must establish the exact symptom and cause and return trusted regression proof, or return a decision-needed packet. Only then may a TDD regression cycle begin.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not mutate production code or claim TDD, RED, cause, or fix from the intermittent report; implementation remains outside this TDD route until the ownership gate is satisfied and authorized.

cases.next_action: Repair the event-loop fixture or test setup, then rerun the focused test.
cases.proof: A valid RED is an observed failure for the expected missing or wrong behavior after setup succeeds.
cases.claim_boundary: The fixture error does not establish RED and supports no TDD progress or behavior claim.

cases.next_action: Record the unrelated failure as a documented pre-existing baseline failure, keep it distinct from the expected focused RED, implement the smallest change, rerun the focused test, and rerun the relevant group.
cases.proof: The focused behavior must pass. Preserve command output showing the nearby failure is the same unrelated baseline issue and record it as residual validation risk.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim that the focused tracer bullet is GREEN, but do not claim full completion under this skill because the nearest relevant group is not passing; disclose the baseline failure and residual risk.

cases.next_action: Preserve the correct retry-count assertion and keep the repository-owned scheduler real behind the tested interface. Use the deterministic scheduler or expose the smallest protected repo-native seam; if that cannot be done safely, return the seam gap as support work.
cases.proof: GREEN must satisfy the original retry-count oracle through the chosen seam with owned in-process behavior real, plus nearby validation. Any double requires the fidelity gate at a real boundary.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not weaken a correct oracle, mock the owned scheduler for convenience, add an unapproved public test hook, or claim success from an implementation-coupled shortcut.

cases.next_action: Extend the boundary fake to preserve the consumed timeout failure mode and, when adapter contract tests exist, run the same contract against the fake and the real or local implementation.
cases.proof: Require matching contract evidence for successful receipt and consumed timeout behavior. If no comparative contract suite exists, record the timeout fidelity as unverified residual risk.
cases.claim_boundary: Without matching timeout proof, claim only successful-receipt coverage; do not claim the fake represents the real queue's timeout behavior.

cases.next_action: Permit only behavior-preserving cleanup inside the tracer-bullet slice, one meaningful move at a time, while GREEN. Stop before the broader seam redesign and return it as residual follow-up; recommend $codebase-design for the already-framed seam question.
cases.proof: Rerun the focused test after every move and the nearest relevant group before leaving the slice.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not widen scope, change the public contract, mutate a tracker, or claim the broader redesign completed; any behavior or interface change requires a new RED cycle.

cases.next_action: Refuse the completion claim and return an incomplete-evidence decision.
cases.proof: The minimum packet is Source Trace with behavior, source, seam, and oracle; observed RED command/result and why it is the expected behavioral failure; observed GREEN command/result; nearby relevant validation or a skipped reason; refactor result or none; and residual risk or blocker.
cases.claim_boundary: Expected RED alone proves neither observed RED nor implementation completion. No TDD completion may be claimed until every implemented behavior crosses observed RED, GREEN, refactor-safe GREEN, and relevant validation.
